steps.title: Find the matching ordered pair
steps.content: With $r=3$ and $s=6$, the pair $(r,s)$ is $(3,6)$. The answer is (B) $(3,6)$.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. The second equation in the given system is $r=3$. Substituting $3$ for $r$ in the first equation in the given system yields $s+7\left(3\right)=27$, or $s+21=27$. Subtracting $21$ from both sides of this equation yields $s=6$. Therefore, the solution $\left(r,s\right)$ to the given system of equations is $\left(3,6\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the solution $\left(s,r\right)$, not $\left(r,s\right)$, to the given system of equations.
